name: North Korea
avoid: Independent travel is not allowed — all sightseeing, meals and transfers must be arranged through a state-approved tour company with guides.
North Korea is currently closed to most tourists, so treat this as reference planning until your operator confirms active departures for your nationality.
July–August bring humid rain and occasional typhoon disruption on the east coast around Wonsan and Mount Kumgang.
stopoverNote: Most classic DPRK trips run 5–7 days from Beijing, Dandong or Vladivostok and stay fully escorted from arrival to departure.

cities.name: Pyongyang
cities.notes: Capital showpiece for Juche Tower, grand monuments, the metro and the tightly choreographed core of most licensed tours.

cities.name: Kaesong
cities.notes: Historic Koryo-era city paired with Panmunjom visits, Confucian academy buildings and old-gate streets near the DMZ.

cities.name: Mount Kumgang
cities.notes: Scenic east-coast mountain zone with guided valley walks, Samilpo Lake and the former inter-Korean tourist village.

cities.name: Wonsan
cities.notes: A controlled east-coast stop for waterfront drives, state beaches, Songdowon camp visits and onward travel to Mount Kumgang.

itinerary.sim: Koryolink tourist SIMs have historically been arranged in Pyongyang for about ₹4,500–₹7,200 plus top-up, but availability is inconsistent and may be suspended while the country remains closed to most tourists.

Pyongyang.name: Pyongyang
Pyongyang.note: Two days covers the core monuments and formal visits; use a third only for slower museum time or a performance/cruise add-on.

days.theme: Arrival monuments, Juche Tower and the formal city core

activities.name: Air Koryo arrival from Beijing or Vladivostok arranged by your operator
Juche Tower observation deck lift
Kim Il Sung Square and Grand People’s Study House exterior circuit
Mansudae Grand Monument bouquet stop

days.theme: Kumsusan protocol, war museum and metro ride

activities.name: Kumsusan Palace of the Sun guided visit
activities.cost: Included in licensed tour; no standalone public ticket

activities.name: Victorious Fatherland Liberation War Museum
Pyongyang Metro two-stop ride
Mangyongdae Schoolchildren’s Palace performance

days.theme: Optional river cruise, gallery time and buffer for state schedule changes

activities.name: Taedong River dinner cruise or boat restaurant supplement
Arch of Triumph and Moran Hill walk
Korean Art Gallery or stamp shop stop

Kaesong.name: Kaesong
Kaesong.note: Kaesong is usually a long escorted day or one-night stop built around Panmunjom and Koryo-era heritage.

days.theme: DMZ/JSA protocol day and Koryo-era history

activities.name: State tour coach from Pyongyang to Kaesong
Panmunjom / DMZ / JSA permit from the North side
Koryo Museum in the old Songgyungwan academy
Kaesong historic district and Nam Gate guided walk
Tomb of King Kongmin

Wonsan.name: Wonsan
Wonsan.note: Wonsan works best as a controlled overnight on the east-coast drive between Pyongyang/Kaesong and Mount Kumgang.

days.theme: East-coast transit, Songdowon visit and waterfront stops

activities.name: State tour coach from Kaesong or Pyongyang corridor to Wonsan
Songdowon International Children’s Camp guided visit
activities.cost: ₹270–₹450 pp when open to foreign groups

activities.name: Jangdok Islet and Wonsan waterfront drive
Kalma coastal promenade photo stop

Mount Kumgang.name: Mount Kumgang
Mount Kumgang.note: One day gives you the classic valley highlights; keep a second only for a cable-car day or slower mountain walks.

days.theme: Tourist village arrival, Inner Kumgang scenery and lake circuit

activities.name: State tour coach from Wonsan to the Mount Kumgang Tourist Region
Inner Kumgang guided hike
Samilpo Lake and tourist village circuit
Daehwa Peak cable car

days.theme: Optional hot-spring or extra mountain day

activities.name: Onjong-ri hot spa or cultural performance supplement
Extra valley walk with state guides
Return coach toward Pyongyang via Wonsan

connections.method: State tour coach arranged by KITC-partner operators (about 2.5–3 hrs)
Escorted coach via Pyongyang corridor (about 6–7 hrs with stops)
Escorted coach along the east coast (about 3 hrs)
State tour coach return via Wonsan (full day drive)

extras: Tour prices are usually quoted in EUR by Koryo Tours, Young Pioneer Tours or KITC partner departures; budget roughly ₹1.5L–₹2.5L pp for a 5–7 day land package before China/Russia positioning flights.
Independent roaming is not permitted: guides accompany you from arrival to departure, and even hotel-area walks can be limited by local security rules.
Kumsusan requires formal clothes (jacket, collared shirt, long trousers or below-knee skirt) and may close without notice for state events.
North Korea remains closed to most tourists as of this planning note; confirm reopening status and nationality eligibility before paying for visas or flights.
Photography rules, phone declarations and checked reading material can change quickly, so follow your guides and tour operator instructions exactly.

Pyongyang: Kim Il Sung Square Pyongyang wide avenue
Kaesong: Panmunjom blue conference huts North Korea side
Kaesong Nam Gate old city walls
Wonsan: Songdowon beach and pine grove Wonsan
Mount Kumgang: Mount Kumgang granite peaks and forest
